chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl. No.2358 of 2024 05.11.2024 Mr. Muhammad Arif, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Khadim Hussain, Addl. P.G. = O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant Muhammad Raza is seeking pre arrest bail in Crime No.448/2024 U/s 489-F PPC of P.S. Pakistan Bazar, Karachi. Applicant is present on interim pre arrest bail granted to him vide order dated 11.10.2024 and matter is fixed for confirmation or otherwise. 2. As per brief facts, complainant invested an amount of Rs.20,00,000/- in the business of applicant against 5% monthly profit. Against such profit, applicant gave him Rs.200,000/- but thereafter stopped making any further payment. On demand, he issued four cheques of Rs.500,000/- each to the complainant which on presentation were dishonored. Hence this case. 3. Learned counsel for applicant and applicant after arguing the case at some length undertake that within two months’ time, the applicant would pay remaining outstanding amount of Rs.18,00,000/- to the complainant, who is also present in person, before the trial court. This proposal has been accepted by the complainant and learned APG. 4. Accordingly, in view of above, the interim pre arrest bail granted to the applicant is confirmed on the same terms and conditions contained in order 11.10.2024. However, applicant shall make payment of remaining amount of Rs.18,00,000/- to the complainant within two months before the trial court. If within two months or on completion of two months, applicant fails to make payment of aforesaid amount to the complainant, his bail would be deemed to have been dismissed and the trial court would be competent to take action against him including his arrest in accordance with law.

However, applicant shall make payment of remaining amount of Rs.18,00,000/- to the complainant within two months before the trial court. If within two months or on completion of two months, applicant fails to make payment of aforesaid amount to the complainant, his bail would be deemed to have been dismissed and the trial court would be competent to take action against him including his arrest in accordance with law. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. J U D G E A.K
